message:
perf(git): per-directory branch info cache (was: single-slot global)

`BRANCH_CACHE` was a `RefCell<Option<CachedBranch>>` — a single slot
keyed implicitly by whatever `cached.working_dir` happened to be on
the most recent insert. Any time `get_branch_info()` was called from
two different working directories within the cache TTL (e.g. the hook
fans out to `git rev-parse` for both the user's repo and a referenced
submodule), the second lookup invalidated the first and the cache hit
rate collapsed to ~0%.

src/git.rs:
- Switch the thread-local cache to
  `RefCell<HashMap<PathBuf, CachedBranch>>`. The const initializer is
  removed because `HashMap::new()` is not const-fn-callable here,
  which is fine — the lazy init runs once per thread on first
  access.
- Extract a new `cached_branch_info_for_dir(current_dir, fetch)`
  helper. `get_branch_info` becomes a thin wrapper that pulls
  `current_dir` from `std::env::current_dir()` and passes
  `fetch_branch_info` as the `fetch` closure. The helper:
  1. Looks up the directory in the map; if found and `is_valid`,
     returns the cached `BranchInfo`.
  2. Otherwise calls `fetch()` exactly once and inserts the result
     under `current_dir`.
  Tests can pass a custom `fetch` closure with a counter to assert
  cache hit/miss behavior.
- `clear_cache()` now calls `HashMap::clear()` instead of dropping
  the only `Some(_)` value.
- Two new tests:
  - `test_branch_cache_keeps_entries_per_directory` — fetches
    `dir_a` then `dir_b` then `dir_a` again, expects 2 fetches total
    and the third call to come from the cache (panic-on-fetch
    closure).
  - `test_branch_cache_refreshes_expired_entries` — pre-populates an
    entry with `cached_at` set to `now - (CACHE_TTL + 1s)`, asserts
    that the next call fetches fresh and the call after that is
    cached again.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
